raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint against Barclays Card Services regarding their handling of a billing dispute ( Case ID # XXXX ) on my credit card ending in XXXX. 

I initially opened a dispute for a charge of {$210.00} from XXXX, as I was entitled to a full refund under Amazons stated policy. XXXX confirmed that a refund was due but failed to process it within their required timeframe ( 30 days ). 

Barclays conducted an investigation but ultimately closed the case in the merchants favor, holding me liable for the charge. 

On XX/XX/year>, I submitted written documentation directly from XXXX confirming that a refund was owed. This was critical evidence that directly supports my claim. 

However : Barclays failed to properly consider or include this evidence in their investigation I received a letter dated XX/XX/year>, stating the case was resolved in the merchants favor I was not given a fair opportunity to respond before the case was finalized When I called Barclays on XX/XX/year>, I was told the case is closed and would not be reopened Additionally, this issue is not isolated. There is a broader pattern of XXXX failing to issue timely refunds to consumers, which has led to legal action, including a reported large-scale class action lawsuit seeking significant restitution for affected customers. This further underscores the importance of properly reviewing merchant-side failures rather than defaulting liability to the consumer. 

This is unacceptable. Barclays : Did not conduct a fair or complete investigation Ignored key documentation submitted within a reasonable timeframe Failed to follow proper dispute resolution procedures I am requesting that Barclays : Reopen the investigation immediately Review the evidence submitted on XX/XX/year> Issue a permanent credit of {$210.00} to my account This situation reflects a failure to properly handle a consumer dispute and a lack of due process.
